acceptance_targets: [chapter_15_f01] PASS if the revised placement at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:411, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:473, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:489 all point to one sleeping spot that can still be described in chapter 16 as the place where Clemence put Elise after the truth came out; FAIL if the chapter still places Elise by the wine stair with its own draft/smell logic while the next-scene boundary requires a ration-room alcove.

[chapter_15_f02] PASS if, after revision, no more than two of the cited beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:35, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:109, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:165, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:235, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:379, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:421, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:473 still use the same clipped negation formula, and each revised passage reads as grammatically complete prose rather than a fragment stack; FAIL if three or more of those marked passages still hinge on the same 'Not X' construction.

Fail if those passages still lean on the same token family as their primary emotional engine.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised placement at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:411, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:473, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:489 all point to one sleeping spot that can still be described in chapter 16 as the place where Clemence put Elise after the truth came out; FAIL if the chapter still places Elise by the wine stair with its own draft/smell logic while the next-scene boundary requires a ration-room alcove.
findings.evidence: Lucien orders Elise to sleep in 'the outer gallery by the wine stairs' at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:411, Clemence takes her to that outer-gallery pallet at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:473, and the closing description still anchors her beside the stair draft and wine smell at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:489.
findings.problem: This handoff breaks chapter-boundary continuity. The allowed boundary pack for chapter 16 remembers Clemence placing Elise in a narrow alcove near the ration room after the truth comes out, but this chapter ends by locating her in the outer gallery at the wine stair. Because the next scene's smoke alarm depends on where Elise wakes, the contradiction is spatial, causal, and canonical rather than cosmetic.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the placement beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:411, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:473, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:489 so they describe one location. Easiest repair: move Elise into a narrow alcove adjoining the ration room, still segregated from the women and children, and let the end-of-chapter sensory detail support that location. If the intended location is truly the wine-stair outer gallery, then those same lines must establish how that spot can honestly be recalled in the next chapter as near the ration room, without inventing an off-page relocation.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, after revision, no more than two of the cited beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:35, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:109, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:165, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:235, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:379, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:421, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:473 still use the same clipped negation formula, and each revised passage reads as grammatically complete prose rather than a fragment stack; FAIL if three or more of those marked passages still hinge on the same 'Not X' construction.
findings.evidence: The narration repeats the same clipped negation/contrast construction at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:35, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:109, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:165, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:183, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:235, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:379, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:421, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:473.
findings.problem: The repeated 'Not X. [replacement].' fragment rhythm has become a chapter-level tic. By the second half it starts flattening moments that should feel distinct, and it brushes against the constitution's ban on clipped narration: instead of sounding compressed by pressure, the prose begins to sound mechanically pre-compressed in the same way every time.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:35, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:109, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:165, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:235, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:379, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:421, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:473, keep only the strongest one or two clipped reversals and rewrite the rest into full, scene-specific syntax. Let some turns land through bodily action, some through longer carried thought, and some through dialogue so the chapter stops resolving pressure with one repeated sentence shape.

findings.acceptance_test: Reading snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5020-5162 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5346-5360, the coercion and betrayal should feel grounded in physical and institutional detail rather than repeated accounting abstractions. Fail if those passages still lean on the same token family as their primary emotional engine.
findings.problem: The style-bible-approved accounting lexicon becomes overfitted in this chapter. `Arithmetic`, `useful`, `exact`, `account`, and related ledger language cluster through the Clemence scene, the Vautrin bargain, and the confession fallout (notably around lines 5020-5026, 5092-5162, and 5346-5360). Instead of sharpening Elise's voice, the repetition starts to sound like a checklist of sanctioned motifs, which reduces the emotional singularity of the book's central betrayal chapter.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5020, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5026, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5092, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5104, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5162, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5346 so the chapter keeps its economic intelligence without repeating the approved token family as scene-labeling vocabulary. Shift pressure back into concrete institutional and bodily specifics: the Charenton bill on the desk, the burned fingertips, the newspaper board, Lucien's cough, the outer-gallery pallet, the loss of access to the map and children. Let those facts carry the coercion instead of repeatedly naming the math.

findings.acceptance_test: Reading snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5126-5130, Vautrin's loss of control should register through a distinctive bureaucratic or bodily adjustment that belongs to him alone. Fail if the revised beat still depends on 'went still,' 'went flat,' or a near-identical freeze image.
findings.problem: The same late-book reaction-image habit returns in the Vautrin office: `Vautrin's face went stiller than anger.` Because the manuscript has already used the stillness/flattening construction across multiple chapters, this line no longer reads as a sharp Vautrin-specific tell; it reads as house prose.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the reaction beat at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5126-5130 with a Vautrin-specific shift tied to his register: clipped courtesy, glove handling, voice precision, a bureaucratic change in how he arranges the desk, or some other administratively cruel motion. This chapter should REPLACE the stillness phrasing rather than keep it; the strongest keeper instance of the pattern is already the brass-marker discovery in chapter 14.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the contradiction that tenderness can coexist with surveillance, desire with betrayal, and ideology with vanity. Render violence, sex, illness, and poverty at the scale characters actually experience them. When an edge case appears, choose the more specific and less reputationally safe version if it remains faithful to character, period, and causality.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches bodily danger, sex, grief, class shame, or political cruelty and then retreats into summary, rewrite toward the exact physical and social fact the character cannot evade.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: Moderate to high; contractions are the spoken default, with full forms reserved for period formality, deliberate emphasis, or Vautrin's cultivated precision.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressive surveillance chapters with slower communal or reflective chapters; vary scene count, dialogue density, and whether the turn comes from action, information, dread, or aftermath so no two adjacent chapters run on the same structural rhythm.
prose_style_profile.diction: Historically grounded and unsentimental; plain enough for labor and bureaucracy, lyrical only when perception earns it rather than rhetoric.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third centered on Elise, elastic enough to register class texture, political pressure, and the quarry's collective life without drifting into authorial essay.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, image-bearing sentences broken by hard factual beats at moments of violence, lust, bureaucratic coercion, or shame.
